text:
Barrigen (バリゲン, Barigen) was a Royal Bodyguard for Prince Marayam chosen by Queen Sevanti. He was under a mission from Queen Sevanti to learn Nen from Kurapika within the two-week time limit, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 369 until his demise. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 370 

Appearance

As a Royal Bodyguard, he wore a black suit and a tie. 

Personality

Not much was known about Barrigen's personality, but he seemed delighted to be chosen over Vergei to attend Kurapika's teaching about Nen. 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

In an outlook of Twelfth and Thirteenth Princes' rooms, Barrigen is shown to be in a room over from Prince Marayam and Queen Sevanti. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 368 

Queen Sevanti sends Belerainte and Barrigen to attend Kurapika's two-week Nen training seminar. His orders include learning Nen within the time limit and that it's a royal order. Elated to hear that he was chosen over Vergei, Barrigen motivates himself that he'll seize the chance for sure. 

At Kurapika's request, he states his name and affiliation. The moment Loberry distracts everyone by claiming there's a strange woman, Barrigen's blood drains from his body, causing him to shrivel and perish. Four white snakes called Silent Majority emerge from under Barrigen's clothes, and Sakata fires at the white snakes, destroying them. Immediately Belerainte examines his body and claims he could have been still alive, but Sakata's bullets certainly killed him. Sakata refutes the claim, arguing he was obviously dead from the snake attack. 

When Sakata contacts Chief Justice Cleapatro to have Loberry arrested for suspicion of Barrigen's murder. Sakata states his suspicions that Loberry killed Barrigen after her distracting outburst. Upon reaching contact with Cleopatro, he requested that Queen Sevanti be arrested as an accomplice of Barrigen's murder and the attempted assassination of the Fourteenth Prince. However, Cleapatro denies the request, arguing how easy circumstantial evidence is to fabricate, and instead orders a seventy-two-hour monitorship of Room 1010. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371
